title: rubus rivularis var. leptobelus
intro: Rubus rivularis var. leptobelus, also known as the stream bramble or river thimbleberry, is a rare and endangered variety of the Rubus genus, characterized by its unique thimble-shaped fruit and specific habitat requirements. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus rivularis var. leptobelus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a valuable addition to holistic health practices. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves that can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
